mergeStrategy: Merge to main after MILESTONE-FINAL passes. Squash commits optional.
description: Phase 2: Decompose Tier 2 loop_engine files (claude.rs 1181L, status.rs 1236L, archive.rs 1213L, calibrate.rs 1568L, detection.rs 1013L). Mechanical extraction — no behavior changes. Continues on the same branch as Phase 1.

priorityPhilosophy.description: The hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN - Map call graphs and test associations before moving any code
2. FUNCTIONING CODE - Extraction compiles and all existing tests pass
3. CORRECTNESS - No orphaned tests, no broken imports, identical test count
4. CODE QUALITY - Module doc comments, pub(crate) visibility, alphabetical mod declarations
5. POLISH - Clean up unused imports in source file after extraction
principles: Move complete call chains together — if A calls B, extract both to same module
Tests move with their tested functions — never orphan a test
Verify cargo test count before and after each extraction
Use pub(crate) for internal APIs, pub only for cross-crate visibility

globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks
criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy` output
Rust: All tests pass with `cargo test`
Test count is identical before and after extraction (run `cargo test 2>&1 | grep 'test result'`)
No breaking changes to existing public APIs — re-export if necessary
Every new module file has a //! module doc comment

priorityGuidelines.medium: 21-50: Fix in current phase if time permits

userStories.title: Baseline test count and verify post-Phase-1 state
userStories.description: Capture the baseline test count after Phase 1 extractions and verify all checks pass. This is the reference point for Phase 2.
acceptanceCriteria: Run `cargo test 2>&1 | grep 'test result'` and record exact counts in progress.txt
Run `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` and confirm zero warnings
Run `cargo fmt --check` and confirm clean
Record baseline line counts: `wc -l src/loop_engine/claude.rs src/loop_engine/status.rs src/loop_engine/archive.rs src/loop_engine/calibrate.rs src/loop_engine/detection.rs`
Audit constants: grep for `const ` in all 5 target files — record which are used by functions being extracted
Audit #[allow] annotations in all 5 target files
Verify Phase 1 modules exist: git_reconcile.rs, output_parsing.rs, prd_reconcile.rs, prompt_sections/, worktree.rs
Audit #[cfg(unix)] / #[cfg(not(unix))] paired blocks in claude.rs — record line numbers and pairing in progress.txt
userStories.notes: Phase 2 starts from the post-Phase-1 state. Every subsequent extraction must match this baseline test count exactly. P1 baseline for reference: 2262 passed, 0 failed, 7 ignored; 23 pub mod declarations in mod.rs. P1 learning: also audit pub(super) visibility patterns — P1 found that some fns needed pub(crate) after extraction.
edgeCases: Phase 1 may have left pending cleanup — verify no leftover TODOs in engine.rs, prompt.rs, env.rs
Some tests may be #[ignore] — count those separately
If Phase 1 is not yet complete on the branch, document which Phase 1 tasks are done
P1 learning: check for duplicate test helpers (e.g. setup_git_repo was duplicated in worktree.rs vs test_utils.rs)
invariants: Total test count must not decrease after any extraction
Zero clippy warnings must be maintained throughout

failureModes.cause: Phase 1 left broken state on branch
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Document in progress.txt and proceed only if cargo test passes

userStories.title: Extract watchdog from claude.rs to watchdog.rs
userStories.description: Move subprocess timeout monitoring and process kill logic from claude.rs to a new src/loop_engine/watchdog.rs module. Functions to extract: watchdog_loop (lines 290-349 unix, 351-384 non-unix), kill_process_group (line 248), exit_code_from_status (line 226), TimeoutConfig struct and from_difficulty (line 42). Keep in claude.rs: spawn_claude (the public entry point). The two #[cfg(unix)] inline blocks inside spawn_claude (lines 124-133 setpgid, lines 159-166 tcsetpgrp) stay with spawn since they are spawn setup, not watchdog logic.
acceptanceCriteria: watchdog.rs contains: TimeoutConfig (struct + from_difficulty), watchdog_loop (both #[cfg] variants), kill_process_group, exit_code_from_status
watchdog.rs has //! module doc comment
All platform-specific #[cfg(unix)] / #[cfg(not(unix))] paired blocks for watchdog stay together in watchdog.rs
The two #[cfg(unix)] inline blocks inside spawn_claude (setpgid, tcsetpgrp) remain in claude.rs
claude.rs imports TimeoutConfig and watchdog_loop from watchdog module
spawn_claude remains in claude.rs as the public entry point — external callers (engine.rs, curate/enrich.rs, learnings/ingestion/mod.rs) need no changes
External callers of TimeoutConfig (engine.rs:435) updated to import from watchdog
Grep for all `TimeoutConfig` usages across codebase and update any additional import paths
Associated tests moved to watchdog.rs #[cfg(test)] module
mod.rs updated with `pub mod watchdog;` in alphabetical order
cargo test count matches baseline
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes
userStories.notes: spawn_claude calls watchdog_loop by spawning a thread — the interface is the function signature. After extraction, claude.rs calls watchdog::watchdog_loop(...) in the spawned thread. TimeoutConfig is used by engine.rs directly (line 435) — so it must be pub, and engine.rs import path changes from claude::TimeoutConfig to watchdog::TimeoutConfig. The #[cfg(unix)] test helpers (lines 605, 625, 665, 1073, 1105) that test watchdog behavior should move too. P1 learnings: (1) Preserve any #[allow] annotations when moving code. (2) Use pub(crate) for internal fns, pub only for cross-module (TimeoutConfig needs pub since engine.rs uses it). (3) Line numbers shift after each removal — verify line numbers before each edit. (4) Keep #[cfg(unix)]/#[cfg(not(unix))] pairs together (P1 kept platform pairs in worktree.rs successfully).

correctness: Test count identical before and after
Platform-specific pairs (#[cfg(unix)] + #[cfg(not(unix))]) both present in watchdog.rs
No orphaned test helpers — unix test primitives move with watchdog tests
style: pub(crate) for watchdog_loop and kill_process_group; pub for TimeoutConfig (used outside loop_engine)

userStories.title: Split status.rs queries from rendering
userStories.description: Separate data-fetching functions from formatting functions in status.rs. Option A: create status/ subdirectory with queries.rs and display.rs. Option B: create two flat files status_queries.rs and status_display.rs. Keep show_status orchestrator in status.rs (or status/mod.rs). Query functions (lines 198-421): read_task_prefix_from_prd, query_project_info, query_dashboard_task_counts, query_pending_tasks, query_distinct_prefixes, read_active_lock_prefix, read_deadline_info, read_single_deadline, prd_basename_from_path. Render functions (lines 432-566): format_text, format_remaining, progress_bar, status_icon.
acceptanceCriteria: Query functions separated from render functions into distinct modules
show_status orchestrator imports from both modules
Each new module has //! doc comment
External callers updated: main.rs (show_status), engine.rs (read_task_prefix_from_prd), handlers.rs (DashboardResult, format_text)
Structs (DashboardResult, ProjectInfo, TaskCounts, PendingTask, DeadlineInfo) remain in status.rs and are re-exported; both status_queries.rs and status_display.rs import from super
Associated tests moved with their functions
mod.rs updated
cargo test count matches baseline
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes
userStories.notes: Prefer flat files (status_queries.rs + status_display.rs) since there are only 2 modules — no need for a subdirectory. The structs (DashboardResult etc.) are used by both layers and by external callers (handlers.rs) — keep them in status.rs and re-export, or move to a shared types location. format_text is pub and called from handlers.rs — its path must remain accessible. read_task_prefix_from_prd is pub and called from engine.rs. P1 learnings: (1) P1 used re-exports successfully (prompt.rs re-exports PromptResult, BuildPromptParams used by engine.rs). Follow same pattern for DashboardResult etc. (2) Import graph must remain a clean DAG — no cycles between status_queries and status_display. (3) Constants stay in their original file (prompt.rs kept TASK_CONTEXT_BUDGET etc.).

correctness: Test count identical before and after
All external callers compile without path changes (use re-exports if needed)
Structs accessible to both query and render modules
style: Query functions: take Connection or Path, return data structs
Render functions: take data structs, return String — pure, no I/O

userStories.title: Document archive.rs learning pipeline and extract formatting
userStories.description: US-008 (deduplicate archive learning extraction) investigation revealed that archive.rs::extract_learnings_from_progress and learnings/ingestion/mod.rs::extract_learnings_from_output are NOT duplicates — they serve different pipelines (markdown progress parsing vs LLM-based extraction). Instead: (1) Document this architectural finding in progress.txt. (2) Extract the formatting/display functions from archive.rs to reduce its size. Functions to extract: format_text (line 173, ~65 lines), and any helper formatters. Keep the core archive logic (run_archive, discovery, clearing, learning extraction) together since they form a cohesive pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: Document in progress.txt: archive.rs learning extraction vs learnings/ingestion are separate pipelines (different inputs, different outputs, not duplicates)
format_text and any formatting helpers extracted to a separate location (archive_display.rs or inline into handlers.rs if it's the only consumer besides main.rs)
Core archive pipeline (run_archive, extract_learnings_from_progress, append_learnings_to_file, discover/clear functions) remains cohesive in archive.rs

PRD US-008 superseded — document that archive.rs extract_learnings_from_progress and learnings/ingestion extract_learnings_from_output are separate pipelines (markdown parsing vs LLM extraction, different inputs/outputs). No deduplication needed. PRD open question #2 resolved.
External callers updated: handlers.rs (ArchiveResult, format_text)
If splitting: verify all external callers compile before committing
Associated tests moved with extracted functions
cargo test count matches baseline
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes
userStories.notes: This task is smaller than originally scoped. The PRD assumed code duplication between archive.rs and learnings/ingestion/, but exploration showed they serve different purposes: archive parses formatted markdown from progress.txt, ingestion uses an LLM on raw Claude output. The main extraction opportunity is format_text (~65 lines) which reduces archive.rs but isn't a major win. If archive.rs is under 1200 lines after documentation only, output 'EXPLICIT_SKIP: archive.rs under 1200 lines, format_text extraction not warranted' in progress.txt and mark complete. P1 learning: P1 had similar pragmatic skip decisions — small extractions (<50 lines) were documented but not extracted.

correctness: Test count identical before and after
style: //! module doc comment if new file created

userStories.title: Extract calibrate.rs statistical math to calibrate_math.rs
userStories.description: Move pure statistical computation functions from calibrate.rs to a new src/loop_engine/calibrate_math.rs module. Functions to extract: compute_correlation (line 365, ~40 lines — Pearson-like point-biserial correlation), adjust_weight (line 405), clamp_weight (line 63), clamp_negative_weight (line 70), and the clamp_to_bounds method on SelectionWeights (line 52). Keep in calibrate.rs: all DB query functions, orchestrators (recalibrate_weights, compute_calibrated_weights), store_weights, load_* functions, and the SelectionWeights struct definition.
acceptanceCriteria: calibrate_math.rs contains: compute_correlation, adjust_weight, clamp_weight, clamp_negative_weight
calibrate_math.rs has //! module doc comment
SelectionWeights::clamp_to_bounds method stays in calibrate.rs (it's a method on the struct) but calls clamp_weight/clamp_negative_weight from calibrate_math
calibrate.rs imports from calibrate_math — no logic duplication
TaskOutcome struct accessible to calibrate_math (either stays in calibrate.rs with pub(crate) or moves to calibrate_math)
Associated tests for pure math functions moved to calibrate_math.rs #[cfg(test)]
mod.rs updated with `pub mod calibrate_math;`
cargo test count matches baseline
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes
userStories.notes: compute_correlation takes &[TaskOutcome] — the TaskOutcome struct must be visible to calibrate_math.rs. Options: (1) keep TaskOutcome in calibrate.rs as pub(crate), import in calibrate_math; (2) move TaskOutcome to calibrate_math since it's a data-only struct used primarily by the math functions. Prefer option 1 since TaskOutcome is populated by DB queries in calibrate.rs. The SelectionWeights struct stays in calibrate.rs since it's used by external callers (selection.rs, engine.rs). P1 learnings: (1) Constants stay in their original file — calibrate.rs constants should remain there. (2) P1 kept shared types in the parent module (prompt.rs kept PromptResult) — follow same pattern for TaskOutcome/SelectionWeights. (3) Verify line numbers before each edit — they shift after removals.

correctness: Test count identical before and after
Pure math functions are truly pure — no Connection parameter, no I/O
External callers (selection.rs, engine.rs) compile without changes
style: Pure functions in calibrate_math — no side effects, easily unit testable

userStories.title: Extract detection.rs exit-code classification
userStories.description: Move exit-code classification from detection.rs to improve separation. The only pure exit-code function is categorize_crash (line 100, ~18 lines) which maps i32 → CrashType. All other functions are output-string analyzers. Given the small extraction size, consider whether a separate module is warranted vs. just improving internal organization with doc comments and section markers. If extracting: create exit_codes.rs with categorize_crash and the CrashType enum. If not extracting: add clear section markers (// --- Exit Code Classification --- and // --- Output String Analysis ---) and document the decision.
acceptanceCriteria: Either: categorize_crash and CrashType extracted to exit_codes.rs with //! doc comment, detection.rs imports them, mod.rs updated, tests moved
Or: detection.rs reorganized with clear section markers and doc comments, decision documented in progress.txt as 'extraction not warranted — single 18-line function'
Decision heuristic: if detection.rs is under 1100 lines, reorganize in-place; if over, extract to exit_codes.rs
In either case: cargo test count matches baseline
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes
userStories.notes: This is the smallest extraction in Phase 2. categorize_crash is 18 lines and has one caller (analyze_output in the same file). The CrashType enum is used by engine.rs (via IterationOutcome). Creating a whole module for 18 lines + an enum may be over-engineering. The agent should make a judgment call: if the team values consistent extraction patterns, do it; if pragmatism wins, organize in-place and document. Either outcome satisfies the AC.

correctness: Test count identical before and after
CrashType enum accessible to all current consumers

userStories.description: Review all 5 extraction tasks for: correct visibility, no orphaned tests, no unused imports, module doc comments present, consistent patterns, integration wiring.
acceptanceCriteria: Each new module has //! doc comment

userStories.title: Review Phase 2 extractions for refactoring opportunities

acceptanceCriteria: No duplicated test helper code across new modules
Import graph is clean
Extraction patterns consistent with Phase 1 modules (git_reconcile.rs, output_parsing.rs, etc.)
Any issues found have corresponding REFACTOR-1-xxx tasks added to JSON
userStories.notes: For each issue found: 1) Add REFACTOR-1-xxx task (priority 18-19), 2) Add to MILESTONE-1 dependsOn, 3) Commit JSON changes.

userStories.title: Update Phase 3 tasks based on Phase 2 extraction learnings

Update tasks/large-file-refactor-p3.json: adjust notes, add edge cases, update touchesFiles if Phase 2 changed structure, refine AC based on what worked. Phase 2 covers smaller files than Phase 1 — patterns around EXPLICIT_SKIP decisions and small-file pragmatism are especially relevant for Phase 3.
acceptanceCriteria: Read progress.txt for all Phase 2 learnings
Review each Phase 3 task and update based on learnings
If Phase 2 established new patterns (e.g., section markers vs extraction for small files), ensure P3 tasks reflect this
Document which P3 tasks were updated and why in progress.txt
